# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

# Use an appropriate base image for your application
FROM alpine:latest

# Set the working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Create directories for logs and cache
# Use /var for logs as it's a standard location, and /tmp for temporary cache
RUN mkdir -p /var/log/myapp /tmp/myapp-cache

# Copy application files (replace with your actual application files)
# Example: COPY . .
# For this example, let's create a dummy app file
RUN echo "echo 'Hello from my app!'" > myapp.sh
RUN chmod +x myapp.sh

# Set directory permissions
# Using 777 for maximum flexibility during development/debugging as requested
# In production, you would typically restrict these much more (e.g., 755 or 770)
RUN chmod -R 777 /var/log/myapp /tmp/myapp-cache

# Example: Run your application (replace with your actual entrypoint)
# This will write a log message to demonstrate write permissions
CMD /bin/sh -c "./myapp.sh; echo 'App started successfully. Logging...' >> /var/log/myapp/app.log; sleep infinity"
